inputs.name: functionalAlias
inputs.description: Name of the Integration Server functional alias for which you want to obtain JDBC information.

inputs.name: getConnectionWait
inputs.description: Optional. Number of milliseconds the service will wait to obtain a connection to a JDBC connection pool. The default is 10 seconds (10000 milliseconds). If a connection is not obtained within this time, the service ends with a status of fail.

outputs.name: minConnections
outputs.description: The minimum number of connections the pool can have.

outputs.name: maxConnections
outputs.description: The maximum number of connections the pool can have.

outputs.name: totalConnections
outputs.description: The total number of connections currently in the pool. This number includes connections that are already in use and connections that are available for use.

outputs.name: availableConnections
outputs.description: The number of connections that are available for use.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: A status indicating whether the service was able to connect to the pool. A value of: Active indicates that the service was able to connect to the JDBC pool. Inactive indicates that the service was not able to connect to the JDBC pool or an invalid functional alias was specified. If the attempt to connect to the JDBC pool failed with a SQLException, the service might throw an exception. If the service does not throw an exception, it will return a message in the message variable.
